title: What Is Hacking?
summary: Learn the builder meaning of hacking, safe breaking, project curiosity, and modern penetration testing.

summary: Move from basic packet viewing into deeper protocol review, evidence cleanup, and troubleshooting.

title: What Is an API?
summary: Learn how requests, responses, JSON, status codes, and API keys fit into small coding projects.

summary: Use VS Code as a project workspace for files, terminal commands, Git, extensions, and remote SSH work.

title: Certification Roadmap
summary: Compare beginner-friendly certifications for networking, Linux, cloud, cybersecurity, and IT fundamentals.

title: Use Libby With Your Library
summary: Use a library card for free ebooks and audiobooks as a practical learning resource.

summary: Start with simple JSON files, then turn them into a real web app running on a Linux server with Flask, Git, Gunicorn, systemd, and Apache or Nginx.

title: JSON Book Recommendations

summary: A meta-project showing how LaunchShell itself was created, organized, versioned, deployed, reviewed, and improved.

summary: Capture private lab traffic with a Raspberry Pi and Alfa Wi-Fi adapter, then review packet evidence.

summary: GitHub Student Developer Pack, Codespaces, Cloudflare, AWS, Termius, Hack The Box Academy, and other tools worth exploring.

summary: A curated Top 30 reading list for cybersecurity, Linux, networking, programming, AI, and systems thinking.

summary: A beginner-friendly route through Linux, GitHub, SSH, cloud servers, Python tools, and safe cybersecurity labs.

summary: The story behind LaunchShell as a second-career student portfolio, project journal, and practical learning resource.
